news_articles.title: Temporary Road Closure on Main Street March 5–7
news_articles.summary: Main Street will be closed between 2nd Avenue and 4th Avenue from March 5–7 for utility repairs.
news_articles.body:
To allow crews to complete critical water main repairs, Main Street will be closed to all through traffic between 2nd Avenue and 4th Avenue from Thursday, March 5 at 7:00 AM through Saturday, March 7 at 8:00 PM. Detours will be clearly marked. Local access to businesses will be maintained.

Drivers should follow posted detour routes using Oak Avenue and 5th Street. Transit routes 3 and 7 will be detoured one block north during the road closure. For questions, contact the Transportation Department at 555-0100.
news_articles.author: Transportation Department

news_articles.title: Nighttime Road Closure on Pine Street February 28
news_articles.summary: Pine Street will have a nighttime road closure between 3rd Avenue and 5th Avenue for paving work.
news_articles.body:
The City will close Pine Street between 3rd Avenue and 5th Avenue on Saturday, February 28 from 8:00 PM to 6:00 AM for final paving work. This nighttime road closure is intended to minimize daytime traffic impacts.

On-street parking will be restricted in the work zone starting at 6:00 PM. Please move parked vehicles before that time. Access for emergency vehicles will be maintained at all times.
news_articles.author: Public Works Department

news_articles.title: Lane Closures on River Bridge for Inspection
news_articles.summary: Single-lane closures on the River Bridge will occur February 20–22 for a routine safety inspection.
news_articles.body:
Drivers should expect intermittent single-lane closures on the River Bridge from Friday, February 20 through Sunday, February 22 between 9:00 AM and 3:00 PM. Flaggers will be present to direct traffic. No full road closure is planned, but delays of up to 15 minutes are possible.

Cyclists and pedestrians will still be able to use the bridge during the inspection.
news_articles.author: Transportation Department

news_articles.title: City Issues Winter Weather Safety Tips
news_articles.summary: Residents are encouraged to prepare for possible snow and ice later this week.
news_articles.body: With a winter storm in the forecast, the City is reminding residents to drive carefully, clear sidewalks, and check on neighbors.
news_articles.author: Emergency Management

news_articles.title: Greenway Trail Segment Temporarily Closed for Tree Work
news_articles.summary: A portion of the Greenway Trail near Oak Park will close for two days for tree maintenance.
news_articles.body: Parks crews will remove damaged trees along the Greenway Trail near Oak Park on February 12–13. The trail segment will be closed between 8:00 AM and 4:00 PM each day.
news_articles.author: Parks & Recreation Department

news_articles.title: Downtown WinterFest Returns February 21
news_articles.summary: The annual WinterFest celebration will feature live music, food trucks, and family activities.
news_articles.body: WinterFest will take place on Saturday, February 21 from 11:00 AM to 4:00 PM in the downtown plaza. Admission is free.
news_articles.author: Cultural Affairs Office

news_articles.title: City Releases Proposed FY 2027 Budget
news_articles.summary: The proposed FY 2027 budget is now available for public review.
news_articles.body: The City Manager has released the proposed FY 2027 operating and capital budget. Residents can review the document online and submit comments.
news_articles.author: City Manager's Office

news_articles.title: Emergency Alert System Test Scheduled for March 6
news_articles.summary: The City will conduct a test of its emergency alert system at noon on March 6.
news_articles.body: Residents who are subscribed to emergency alerts will receive a test message by text and email. No action is required.
news_articles.author: Emergency Management

news_articles.title: Community Pool to Open Early for Spring Break
news_articles.summary: The community pool will open one week early with extended swim hours during spring break.
news_articles.body: To accommodate families during spring break, the community pool will open on March 15 with additional lap swim and open swim sessions.
news_articles.author: Parks & Recreation Department

news_articles.title: City Offices Closed for Presidents Day
news_articles.summary: City administrative offices will be closed on February 16 in observance of Presidents Day.
news_articles.body: Essential public safety and utility services will operate as usual. Trash and recycling collection will occur one day later than normal for the remainder of the week.
news_articles.author: Communications Office

news_articles.title: March First Friday Art Walk Preview
news_articles.summary: Galleries and arts organizations will host extended hours and special programs on March 6.
news_articles.body: The March First Friday Art Walk will feature over 20 participating venues, live performances, and public art tours.
news_articles.author: Cultural Affairs Office

news_articles.title: Sidewalk Closure on 8th Avenue Near School
news_articles.summary: A short-term sidewalk closure will affect pedestrians near Lincoln Elementary.
news_articles.body: Contractors will close the east-side sidewalk of 8th Avenue between Maple Street and Birch Street from February 17–19. A signed pedestrian detour will be provided on the west side of the street.
news_articles.author: Public Works Department

news_articles.title: New Traffic Signal Activated at Oak Avenue and 10th Street
news_articles.summary: A new signalized intersection is now active to improve safety for drivers and pedestrians.
news_articles.body: The new traffic signal at Oak Avenue and 10th Street was activated on February 8 following a period of flashing operation.
news_articles.author: Transportation Department

news_articles.title: Spring Concert Series Lineup Announced
news_articles.summary: The City has announced the lineup for the free outdoor spring concert series at Riverfront Park.
news_articles.body: Concerts will take place every Friday evening in May and June, featuring local and regional performers across a variety of genres.
news_articles.author: Cultural Affairs Office

parks.name: Oak Meadow Park
parks.description: Neighborhood park with a large playground, open lawn, picnic shelter, and restrooms.
parks.notes: Playground is recommended for ages 2–12. Restrooms are open seasonally during winter break reduced hours.

parks.name: Riverfront Park
parks.description: Large community park along the river with trails, event lawn, and amphitheater.
parks.notes: Hosts concerts and festivals during spring and summer.

parks.name: Lincoln Playfield
parks.description: Sports-focused park with soccer fields and a small playground.
parks.notes: Portable restrooms may be available during tournament weekends.

parks.name: Cedar Grove Park
parks.description: Shady neighborhood park with walking paths, playground, and permanent restrooms.
parks.notes: Popular for after-school visits; parking lot entrance on Pine Street.

parks.name: Hillside Nature Preserve
parks.description: Natural area with hiking trails and viewpoints. Limited facilities.
parks.notes: No restrooms on site. Dogs must be on leash.

parks.name: Maple Commons
parks.description: Small urban plaza with seating, public art, and a splash pad.
parks.notes: Splash pad operates Memorial Day through Labor Day.

parks.name: Pine View Park
parks.description: Family-friendly park with two playground areas, a picnic pavilion, and accessible restrooms.
parks.notes: Playground surfacing is rubberized and wheelchair accessible.

parks.name: Sunset Fields
parks.description: Multi-field sports complex with evening lighting.
parks.notes: Field use by reservation; restrooms open during scheduled events.

parks.name: Lakeside Park
parks.description: Scenic lake with walking path, fishing pier, and picnic areas.
parks.notes: Fishing license required. Playground located near the north parking lot.

parks.name: Downtown Plaza
parks.description: Central plaza used for markets and community events.
parks.notes: Portable restrooms available during large events only.

reading_lists.description: Default reading list for saved city news articles.

reading_lists.description: Articles related to transportation, construction, and road closures.

reading_lists.name: Parks & Events

reading_lists.name: City Council & Policy
reading_lists.description: Collection of articles and updates on City Council actions and policy changes.

subscription_topics.name: Emergency Alerts
subscription_topics.description: Immediate notifications about life-safety emergencies, severe weather, and major service disruptions.

subscription_topics.name: Arts & Events Updates
subscription_topics.description: Weekly highlights of arts, culture, and community events happening around the city.

subscription_topics.name: General City News
subscription_topics.description: News releases and announcements from city departments.

subscription_topics.name: Traffic & Transit Alerts
subscription_topics.description: Updates on planned road closures, major traffic incidents, and transit detours.

recreation_classes.title: Youth Beginner Swim - Saturday 9:00 AM
recreation_classes.description: Introductory swim class for ages 8–12 focusing on water safety, floating, and basic strokes. Small class size for individualized attention.

recreation_classes.title: Youth Intermediate Swim - Saturday 10:30 AM
recreation_classes.description: For swimmers who are comfortable in deep water and can swim at least 15 yards unassisted. Focus on stroke refinement and endurance.

recreation_classes.title: Parent & Toddler Swim - Saturday 8:30 AM
recreation_classes.description: A fun introduction to the water for ages 2–4 with a caregiver in the pool. Songs, games, and basic safety skills.

recreation_classes.title: Adult Lap Swim Training - Tuesday 6:00 PM
recreation_classes.description: Coach-led lap swim workout for adults of varying skill levels. Focus on technique, pacing, and fitness.

recreation_classes.title: Youth Beginner Swim - Thursday 5:00 PM
recreation_classes.description: Weekday option of youth beginner swim for ages 8–12 covering the same curriculum as the Saturday session.

recreation_classes.title: Family Fitness Fun - Saturday 10:00 AM
recreation_classes.description: All-ages circuit-style class with games and activities designed for families to be active together.

recreation_classes.title: Evening Yoga - Monday 6:30 PM
recreation_classes.description: Gentle yoga class for adults focusing on flexibility, balance, and stress relief. Participants should bring a mat.

recreation_classes.title: Youth Drawing & Painting - Saturday 9:30 AM
recreation_classes.description: Creative art class for ages 9–13 exploring drawing, painting, and mixed media projects.

recreation_classes.title: Adult Pottery Basics - Wednesday 7:00 PM
recreation_classes.description: Hands-on introduction to wheel throwing and hand-building techniques. Clay and firing included.

recreation_classes.title: Youth Soccer Skills - Tuesday 4:00 PM
recreation_classes.description: Fundamental soccer skills and scrimmages for ages 7–11. Shin guards required.

recreation_classes.title: Teen Basketball League - Thursday 7:00 PM
recreation_classes.description: Recreational basketball league for teens with weekly games and supervised open gym.

recreation_classes.title: STEM Explorers - Saturday 11:00 AM
recreation_classes.description: Hands-on science, technology, engineering, and math projects for curious kids ages 8–12.

recreation_classes.title: Digital Literacy for Seniors - Wednesday 10:00 AM
recreation_classes.description: Small-group instruction on using smartphones, email, and online city services safely.

recreation_classes.title: One-Day Family Nature Workshop
recreation_classes.description: Single-session workshop exploring local wildlife and habitats with guided activities in the park.

recreation_classes.title: Youth Beginner Swim - Late Spring Saturday 9:00 AM
recreation_classes.description: Second spring session of youth beginner swim for ages 8–12. Ideal for those continuing from the early spring series.
